Hello friends and welcome to lesson two, which is all about mastering your application questions. So this is really the first major asset we're going to be building out in AFA. So first as a recap, what are you coaching, right? And how are you accomplishing that through your application questions? So first is mastery, which is going to come in a unique form on your application. It's going to come through them getting to reflect on what has resonated about your method. What is giving them confidence that you can be the right fit for them, right? So they're going to be reflecting on these things. They're going to be communicating them based on the questions you ask. And this is going to have them connecting to your sense of mastery on your topic that gives them trust and excitement. Next will be momentum, right? So momentum in the case of your application is really going to be the time and energy they invest in answering the questions. And this is why it is important to have good questions that require a degree of reflection of time and energy and thought investment to complete. And next will be magnetism. This is going to become apparent through the transparency alerts, which you're going to learn about in just a second. So the high level overview, the frame of mind I like to have as I create application questions is essentially the million dollar bet. So if you had to place a million dollar bet on the applicant succeeding in your program based solely on the info that you'd be getting from the application. So not being on a call, but just from what they fill out here. What would you ask? What would you have them fill out to know that you're making a safe bet? So this is the frame of mind you should have in creating your questions, questions that will reveal to you whether or not they would be a safe bet in getting success in your program. Next is to err on the side of length. So so many people have this fear that people won't take the time to fill out their applications and they try to shorten it as make it as short and least time consuming as possible out of that fear, right? Which doesn't really make sense because you're asking for 15 to 30 minutes of time max, right? And if they enroll in your program, they will be investing hours, maybe dozens of hours in your content or in your coaching. So this is a very small initial investment with you if they are the right fit. And this is truly the start of that coaching relationship. It shouldn't be too great of an ask for that 15 to 30 minutes to be invested. In fact, it's a great way to qualify those who are ready, willing and excited to invest time with you. Moreover, great prospects feel best when they feel they put everything on the table. So your questions should give them a chance to feel like they've put everything on the table for you to consider, right? No one really cares or believes or trusts your prescriptive actions unless they feel they put all their symptoms on the table. And this is why we err on the side of length in our application form. And ultimately, you're going for fewer applicants that are of higher quality, right? So yes, it is true that you may get applicant drop off because eight questions or 12 questions or 15 questions is just too long for them. But if that is what is truly needed for you to feel safe in inviting them into your program or getting a good sense of their potential fit, then those probably aren't the best fit of people you want coming in. You want people willing to do that. So people will drop off if you have too many questions, but that's OK. There's no excuse for pumping volume into your application funnel and onto your sales calls. Unless you have a team of sales people that are just hell bent on twisting poor fits into weak yeses, which is not what we're doing here to begin with. And then finally, great questions give you great starting points. So ask questions on your application that will yield answers that you can build immediate connection around on your call, right? How great would it be to start off with, A, this really stood out to me and reflect something that stood out for you for them, right? That is something that we do to create magnetism, rapport, resonance versus all that cheap, rapport building stuff you may have read back in sales books in the 90s, right? And essentially like asking where they're from, how the weather is, if the foliage has changed colors, all these things that are very surfacing. So great questions will give you great, relevant, amazing starting points for your conversation. All right, so we have a 15 point AFA format. These are questions that I like to follow. Of course, you have creative freedom to ask what you need to in service of this million dollar bet. But the first one is essentially, am I in the right place? So call out your segments with a degree of specificity. Next, I like to get a recency snapshot as it relates to the goal they're trying to attain or the problem they're trying to solve. Next question, I love connecting them to their power and resourcefulness, essentially reflecting on something they could feel good and proud about. And next, we start getting a little bit more specific what they'd like to achieve with you or your program, any problems, pitfalls, they've been experiencing, things that they want fixed. So this is more problem focused. And then it is about reflecting mastery, right? So at this point in the application, they should have had some experience with you and your content. And that is why they are applying to work with you in a deeper way. So you want them to connect to those things that they already trust and believe in, right? So, Asim, what gives them confidence that you specifically, so that once again is magnetism, you specifically can help with? What gives them confidence in your unique process and method? Have they read a PDF, a book? Have they been on a webinar that really outlined your process and method? What has resonated around that? So that is a place for them to reflect on and communicate it. And then finally, we get into some degree of qualification and exclusive empowerment. So we're going to ask three questions that really qualify whether or not they are going to be in a position to get into your program, to succeed with it. And of course, if they do qualify, right, that is going to elicit a degree of exclusive empowerment, a chance that, wow, I am in a unique place to be able to get into this program and succeed through it. So we're going to wrap with three of those. And then some quick hitters. We're going to have an ethos-based question. Ask them if they are in agreement with a certain ethos we have around our business and our way of doing things. And 12, the last one I like to have is essentially any remaining questions that can elicit friction. I like to put those at the end once so much momentum has been built. And we're really at the final question where people are least likely to abandon it if there's something that has a little bit more friction involved. And then finally, we wrap with what I call the three transparency alerts. So the first one is to create ease and safety about the sales call itself. The next is stating your own boundaries to see if they're going to be a match for that. And then the third one, very controversial, but essentially the price range transparency, essentially what is the range of the program that you're going to be offering them, right? And I do this because it creates a sense of mutual confidence. It gives me confidence that someone has consented to that in advance and still wants to explore. The possibility of partnering together. That gives me a lot of confidence in talking to somebody. And of course, they become more receptive to the call themselves. They're not on edge the whole time trying to restrain and restrict their own interests because they're afraid of the implications of what them desiring this would be, which would be involved in them essentially being vulnerable to a price tag that they have no idea what it is yet. So it creates ease and confidence for both parties. But of course, use your discernment. And if that is going to be of service to your audience or not of service. All right. So I'm going to go over some of the copy elements as it relates to our automated intimacy application. And in the video below this, you're actually going to see how it played out in real time. So the first question segmentation bet segmentation based sorry calling out our core segments. What's the best way to describe your awesomeness? Right. So this is all about having them see themselves on the page. We use a degree of specificity here. I'm a bad ass service provider, copyright or automation specialist design looking to level up my skills, differentiate from the crowd and add high profit productized services to my stack. So use quite specific languaging in calling out your segments. Right. Most applications here essentially just say, what is your revenue level in 2021? Which essentially just makes someone feel like they're being qualified based on their finances. We of course want to go significantly deeper and create magnetism in seeing our prospects for who they truly are and what they're trying to achieve. Next is give me a taste of what the last 60 days have looked like in your life and business. So as I mentioned before, I prefer a recent snapshot of where they're at in the process of achieving their goal or overcoming their problem. Nothing particularly wrong with a long view historically, but I do feel in my experience that it's harder for people to recall with a degree of accuracy. But more importantly, it's less reflective of who they actually are coming into the call. I don't really want them talking about something that happened last year when they were in a totally different place in their life and business. I want to know who they are right now, who I am going to be experiencing in the next few days. Next, what is the most exciting, expansive step you've taken in your business over the last month or two? So this is about getting them in a state where they are reflecting on their own power, their own resourcefulness, their own capacities and abilities, and essentially demonstrating to us that they are able and actively committed to taking productive action. If they have done nothing, if there's been nothing exciting or expansive over the last month or two, chances are it's not from a lack of content. It's a lack of commitment or ability. So there should be some degree of momentum. There should be something they can reflect on to feel a sense of pride and power and capacity around. And that's something that I think is vital for them to connect to before getting on the sales call with us. Next, it's going to be their goal unified or tied with our programs. This is very specific language, but what are the most vital, heart-pounding business and life achievements you'd like to whiskey or kombucha clink to us? Kombucha clink to with us. Sorry, that's a mouthful in the next few months. So here we are bridging their goals with our program. So this is creating a future pace where we are essentially marrying their goals with us. It's not just a singular, what are your biggest goals? It's what are your biggest goals inside our program? So very important, little detail there. Next, what do you currently perceive as your biggest missed opportunities, leakages or inefficiencies in your marketing or sales process? So it might not necessarily be a major bleeding neck problem, as you may have learned about in other marketing circles, but essentially missed opportunities and inefficiencies for clients that are already empowered for people who are already in pretty decent shape. You may want to frame the problem as missed opportunities or inefficiencies. Just a little sign out there. And then we move into the two more mastery driven ones where they get to reflect what gives you a sense of confidence that we might be able to help. So of course we want to work with people or we want to get on calls with people who already have a degree of experience with us, with our programs, with our training, with our content and have that sense of confidence. This is a very difficult thing to coach on a sales call, right? It's not impossible, but we want to work with people who've already experienced our mastery on some level and already feel that confidence. Same thing. We want to work with people who've already had a bit of insight into what automated intimacy is. So we're asking a flat out what resonates most about the concept of automated intimacy. And that's going to give them a chance to reflect on their current level of trust and confidence in us. Next, we move into the qualification question. So this is really making sure they are empowered to get the result in the program. So on a scale of one to 10, how confident are you in your or your team's copywriting abilities? Right? A lot of what we teach is going to involve copy. They need to have a degree of confidence in themselves or their team to be able to execute it. Same thing with tech and automation. And then it's just a question, right? Do you have a team supporting you in all these things or are you rocking them so low for now? So these are all about making sure that they have either the skills or the support needed to succeed in the program. And then we move into the ethos question to coach magnetism, right? Do you believe that taking the time to explore the nuances of ethical marketing and sales strategies is a solid use of time? Yes or no, right? This is essentially the core of our business using advanced tools, advanced strategies that actually make ethical marketing effective, right? So this is the core of our business. We want to make sure there's resonance on an ethos level. And then finally, we end on the more friction based question, right? So do you currently use active campaign or if not, would you be open to migrating? So the open to migrating part is where there might be a little bit of pause, a little bit of resistance. So we want that to be last in the sequence. We don't want that to be something that gets in the way of them fully considering and answering the other questions. But yet at the same time, it's an important disclaimer, right? So much of our program is based on active campaign. Of course, it's transferable to other systems, CRMs, email providers. But it's important for us to know if someone would be open to migrating and important for them to know if it's something they see themselves doing. And then finally, we're going to wrap with the three transparency alerts. First one, your call will be either be with either Ry or Phil, the co-founders of automated intimacy. We're not hired gun sales. People are closers. Poor damn good at knowing if they will be the right fit for you because, well, we created it. Created it. Is that cool? Yes or no. So this is all about creating safety in the call itself. Transparency alert number two is our boundaries, right? Making sure that they are going to be the right fit for us. So we do best with self-sufficient entrepreneurs and business owners who don't have some urgent life or death problem that they need us to save them from. Yes, solving problems is cool. And something we're great at, but helping you scale up, season you ops and get from great to epic is where we love to play with us on that. Yes or no. So this is really just our own boundary, our own defense from working with clients who see us as the saviors of their business. Right? We don't want to play in that space. So we're calling it out over here. And then transparency alert number three is the big, crazy controversial one. This is where we essentially put the price range on display in full transparency and make sure that they feel confident about it. So the investment for automated intimacy ranges from four to 15 K, depending on the level of one on one support would most serve you at the stage. If you're solid fit with a clear path to an ROI, are you empowered to make the time, energy and financial investments without stomach clenching fear? Maxing out your credit cards or sacrificing your enjoyment of spot is she had two massages, an organic grass bed steak. Yes or no. So this is both a personal boundary. Right? We don't want people who are making this stomach clenching investment and feel like they have to sacrifice everything in their life. Right? We don't want this to be scary. We want it to be an appropriate investment for the value we are delivering, but we certainly don't want to work with people who are maxing out credit cards. And the process. So we asked this question, where essentially we put this on transparency alert number three, because we want the confidence on the call, knowing that someone is already aware of this and consenting to it. And we believe our prospects, those in our market would feel most comfortable with this transparency and knowing what they're potentially getting themselves into if they move forward. So that is what is going on in the application. I suggest you start with this 15 point application process, write down your questions and of course take creative freedom and liberties where you see fit as long as it is in service of this million dollar bet and three main things we're coaching. So that's all we got here. Take a breather and in the videos below, you will see both a fireside chat and walk. Through of the live version of this application as well as some extra tips on the tech side from Phil. So thanks so much for being here and I'll catch you guys in the next video. Bye for now.
